* [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bb5|Spanish game]] → [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bb5/3...d6|Steinitz defence]] * [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. d4|Scotch game]] → [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...d6/3. d4/3...Nc6|3...d6?!]] (by transposition, rare) * [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...d6|Philidor defence]] → [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...d6/3. d4/3...Nc6|3...Nc6?!]] (by transposition, rare) }} == 4. d4 == White has two attackers on e5 and threatens to win it. Black must either take the pawn first in trade, [[/4...exd4|'''4...exd4''']] 5. Nxd4, or break the pin with [[/4...Bd7|'''4...Bd7''']], so defending e5. In the latter continuation, Black may find themselves choosing between keeping the pawn protected and their development. [[/4...Nf6|'''4...Nf6?!''']], counter-attacking e4, doesn't work out due to 5. dxe5 Nxe4 6. Qd5!, attacking the knight on e4. If Black defends well, they only lose a pawn: 6...Nc5 7. Ng5 (threatening Qxf7#) Ne6 (interposes between the queen and f7) 8. Bxc6+ bxc6 9. Qxc6+ (wins a pawn) Bd7 10. Qf3 (threatens Qxf7#) Nxg5 11. Bxg5 (trades knights){{Chess/not|+}}. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}} :'''1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. Bb5 d6 4. d4 '''
